channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Travelodge updates room key procedures following guest assault
item.description: Travelodge has announced immediate changes to its room key policy after a woman was sexually assaulted by a man who had been given a key card to her room. The attacker, Kyran Smith, was jailed for sev...

item.title: Ontario court decision highlights estate challenges when cottage value exceeds heir’s share
item.description: A recent Ontario Superior Court of Justice ruling in Haddock v. Haddock demonstrates the complications that can arise when a will does not clearly address the distribution of a property that has signi...

item.title: Jailed PTI Leaders Urge Chief Justice for Early Hearing of Long-Pending Appeals
item.description: Several PTI leaders, currently serving sentences for their involvement in the May 9, 2023 riots, have written to the Chief Justice of Pakistan, Justice Yahya Afridi, requesting an expedited hearing of...

item.title: Second death confirmed in Kent meningitis outbreak as school pupil dies
item.description: A sixth-form pupil at Queen Elizabeth's Grammar School in Faversham has died from meningitis, becoming the second fatality in an ongoing outbreak in the Canterbury area of Kent. The student, in Year 1...

item.title: Highlights and Historic Moments from the 2026 Oscars Ceremony
item.description: The 2026 Oscars celebrated cinematic achievements with major wins, historic firsts, and memorable moments. 'One Battle After Another' dominated the night with six awards, including Best Picture, Best ...

item.title: ACMA imposes strict conditions on Kyle and Jackie O ahead of any potential radio return
item.description: The Australian Communications and Media Authority (ACMA) has set out new, stricter conditions for ARN’s KIISFM stations should Kyle Sandilands or Jackie 'O' Henderson return to the airwaves, whether i...

item.title: Lifezone inks 14-month agreement with Burundi to explore Musongati Nickel Deposit
item.description: Lifezone Metals, which owns Tembo Nickel, has entered a 14-month exclusivity agreement with the government of Burundi to explore the Musongati Nickel Deposit. This project is expected to expand Tanzan...

item.title: Grammy Winner Lalah Hathaway to Headline Inaugural Montreux Jazz Festival in Franschhoek
item.description: Grammy-winning American singer Lalah Hathaway is set to perform in South Africa at the inaugural Montreux Jazz Festival Franschhoek from 27 to 29 March 2026. She will be joined by acclaimed pianist an...

item.title: CARA Issues Directives to States on Adoption Compliance and Child Identity Protection
item.description: The Central Adoption Resource Authority (CARA), India’s apex body for adoptions, has issued three office memorandums to all states and Union Territories to ensure strict adherence to adoption procedur...

item.title: Profile of the Sawiris Brothers: Egypt's Leading Business Family and Their Global Ventures
item.description: The Sawiris brothers from Egypt – Nassef, Naguib, and Samih – are among Africa's richest individuals, collectively owning assets worth over $16 billion (around KSh 2.07 trillion). Sons of the late Ons...

item.title: Norwegian Director Joachim Trier Reflects on Family, Punk Roots and Filmmaking
item.description: Joachim Trier, the Norwegian filmmaker recently awarded an Oscar for his latest film 'Sentimental Value', has a colourful past that shaped his distinctive cinematic voice. In his youth, Trier was deep...

item.title: Mother and Sons Arrested for Running Illegal Drug Den in Maguindanao del Norte
item.description: Authorities in Datu Odin Sinsuat, Maguindanao del Norte arrested a mother and her two sons on the night of March 15 for operating a clandestine drug den. The suspects were caught selling methamphetami...

item.title: Highlights from the Oscars red carpet: stylish and vibrant celebrity looks
item.description: At the 98th Academy Awards, celebrities brought a mix of modernity and bold colours to the red carpet, showcasing both contemporary designs and timeless elegance. Notable winners of the night included...

item.title: Drone Strikes Hit UAE Amid Iran-US Conflict; Trump Pressures NATO Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: On 16 March 2026, Iran launched new drone attacks on the United Arab Emirates, striking a fuel tank near Dubai International Airport and causing a fire at a major oil terminal in Fujairah. The airport...

item.title: Analysis of Tanzania’s Digital Payments Reveals Growing Economic Activity
item.description: New transaction data from Tanzania dey show say the economy dey mature and dey grow in different ways. Between 2024 and 2025, the number of payments small small rise from 490,000 to 523,000, but the v...

item.title: COMELEC Reports Over 3.1 Million Voter Registrations for 2026 Barangay and SK Elections as of Mid-March
item.description: The Commission on Elections (COMELEC) announced that more than 3.1 million Filipinos have already registered to vote for the 2026 Barangay and Sangguniang Kabataan Elections (BSKE). As of March 14, 20...

item.title: Deve Gowda expresses concern over informal protest practices in Parliament to Sonia Gandhi
item.description: Former Prime Minister H. D. Deve Gowda has written a letter to Congress president Sonia Gandhi expressing his concern regarding the prevailing culture of casual protests in the Indian Parliament. Acco...

item.title: Tragic Roof Collapse During BISP Payment Kills Multiple Women in Rahim Yar Khan
item.description: In Rahim Yar Khan district, a tragic accident took place during the disbursement of Benazir Income Support Programme (BISP) payments when the roof of a local shop collapsed. According to officials, ov...

item.title: High Court Approves Presidential Commission to Probe Post-Election Violence
item.description: Dar es Salaam — The High Court of Tanzania has dismissed a case challenging the President's decision to set up a commission of inquiry on violence linked to the October 29, 2025 General Election. Thre...

item.title: Rajya Sabha MP urges Parliament to pass law on end-of-life care for terminally ill patients
item.description: On March 16, 2026, IUML MP Haris Beeran from Kerala urged the Indian government in Rajya Sabha to introduce legislation on end-of-life care for terminally ill patients. He highlighted that despite rep...
